undefined.name: Undefined
undefined.message: Undefined in diagnostic information usually means there is no issue here.
undefined.fixAdvice: You generally don't need to consider this diagnostic message unless you encounter other abnormal behavior.

unknownError.name: Unknown Error
unknownError.message: An unknown error has occurred, possibly due to an internal error or unhandled exception.
unknownError.fixAdvice: Please try restarting ASER Studio. If the problem persists, contact technical support and provide the relevant logs.

ruleNotSelected.name: No Grammar Rule Selected
ruleNotSelected.message: Document %1 does not specify any grammar rule via the #useRule directive.
ruleNotSelected.fixAdvice: Try adding #useRule:%1 on the second line of the document to specify the grammar rule defined in %1.

noSuchRule.name: Grammar Rule Does Not Exist
noSuchRule.message: The grammar rule %2 specified by the #useRule directive in document %1 is invalid.
noSuchRule.fixAdvice: Check the parameter of the #useRule directive in document %1 to ensure it correctly specifies an existing grammar rule. Currently valid rules are: %2

noSuchController.name: Controller Does Not Exist
noSuchController.fixAdvice: Check whether your code correctly references the controller prefix specified by rule %1, ensuring it has been defined and the name is spelled correctly.

missingRequiredParameter.name: Missing Required Parameter
missingRequiredParameter.message: Controller "%1 Controller" is missing the required parameter %2.
missingRequiredParameter.fixAdvice: Remove the symbol indicating optional parameters and refill the value starting from the line identifier.

parameterTypeMismatch.name: Parameter Type Mismatch
parameterTypeMismatch.message: Parameter %1 has a type mismatch. You may have provided type %2, but the expected type is %3.

parameterOutOfRange.name: Parameter Out of Range
parameterOutOfRange.message: The value of parameter %1 is outside the allowed range.
parameterOutOfRange.fixAdvice: Check the controller parameter to ensure its value is within the allowed range.

parameterFormatError.name: Parameter Format Error
parameterFormatError.fixAdvice: Check the controller parameter to ensure its format meets expectations.

unexceptedParameter.name: Unexpected Parameter
unexceptedParameter.fixAdvice: Check the definition of controller %1 to ensure it only accepts expected parameters and no extra parameters are passed.

unclosedSymbol.name: Unclosed Symbol
unclosedSymbol.message: In document %1, symbol %2 is not properly closed.
unclosedSymbol.fixAdvice: Check the symbols %2 in document %1 to ensure they are properly closed. (Add %3 symbols in appropriate places to close them.)

variableNotDefined.name: Variable Not Defined
variableNotDefined.fixAdvice: Please check the spelling of %1, or ensure you have previously defined this variable via the statement %1.

useTabInsteadSpace.name: Use Tab Instead of Space
useTabInsteadSpace.message: Spaces were used where tabs should be used.

useSpaceInsteadTab.name: Use Space Instead of Tab
useSpaceInsteadTab.message: Tabs were used where spaces should be used.
useSpaceInsteadTab.fixAdvice: Replace tabs with the appropriate number of spaces.

useEnglishPunctuation.name: Use English Punctuation
useEnglishPunctuation.message: Chinese punctuation was used where English punctuation should be used.
useEnglishPunctuation.fixAdvice: Replace Chinese punctuation with the corresponding English punctuation.

useChinesePunctuation.name: Use Chinese Punctuation
useChinesePunctuation.message: English punctuation was used where Chinese punctuation should be used.
useChinesePunctuation.fixAdvice: Replace English punctuation with the corresponding Chinese punctuation.

noSuchBlock.name: Block Does Not Exist
noSuchBlock.fixAdvice: Please check the spelling of %1, or ensure you have previously defined this block via the statement %1.

blockNameConflict.name: Block Name Conflict
blockNameConflict.message: Block %1 has already been defined at line %2.
blockNameConflict.fixAdvice: Choose a different name for this block or the block already defined at line %1.

instrumentRedundant.name: Redundant Directive
instrumentRedundant.message: Directive %1 is redundant here; it duplicates the directive at line %1.

unknownWarning.name: Unknown Warning
unknownWarning.message: An unknown warning has occurred, possibly due to an internal error or unhandled exception.
unknownWarning.fixAdvice: Please try restarting ASER Studio. If the problem persists, contact technical support and provide the relevant logs.

nosuchResource.name: Resource Does Not Exist
nosuchResource.fixAdvice: Please check the spelling of %1, or add a resource named %1 to your project.

useTabInsteadSpace_dialog.name: Use Tab Instead of Space in Dialog Controller
useTabInsteadSpace_dialog.message: In dialog controllers, use tabs to separate the speaker from the dialog content instead of spaces. If you are certain that the spaces are part of the character's name, please ignore this warning.

Preprocessor.__description: Preprocessor directives for processing documents during the preparation stage before playback.
Preprocessor.ruleName: Grammar rule name, used in the #useRule directive to define the grammar rule name
Preprocessor.blockName: Block name, used in the #block directive to define a block name, and in the #use directive to specify the block to call
Preprocessor.versionNumber: Version name, used in the #version directive to specify the version of a controller or grammar rule

Background.ImageName: Specifies the image name used for the background
Background.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the background, in seconds
Background.StartPosition: Starting coordinate position for the background image
Background.EndPosition: Ending coordinate position for the background image
Background.MoveTime: Time for the background image to move from the start position to the end position, in seconds
Background.MoveCurve: Curve type for the background image movement
Background.IsBlocker: Whether to block. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line
Background.StitchVertically: Whether to stitch multiple images vertically. True for vertical stitching, false for horizontal stitching.

Music.AudioName: Specifies the audio name used for the music
Music.Volume: Specifies the volume of the music, ranging from 0 to 1
Music.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the music, in seconds
Music.SlotIndex: Specifies the slot index for the music, default is 0 (i.e., track number). To play multiple pieces of music simultaneously, use different slot indices to distinguish them.
Music.Position: Spatial position of the music, -1 for left, 1 for right, freely adjustable within this range

SoundEffect.AudioName: Specifies the audio name used for the sound effect
SoundEffect.Volume: Specifies the volume of the sound effect, ranging from 0 to 1
SoundEffect.Position: Spatial position of the sound effect, -1 for left, 1 for right, freely adjustable within this range
SoundEffect.Steps: Specifies the duration of the sound effect in steps, i.e., how many controllers it lasts before terminating

Character.Character&Variant: Specifies the character name and variant. If there is no variant, use only the character name
Character.SlotIndex: Specifies the slot index for the character, default is 0. To display multiple characters simultaneously, use different slot indices to distinguish them.
Character.IsBlocker: Whether to block. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line
Character.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the character, in seconds
Character.FadeOutTime: Specifies the fade-out time for the character, in seconds
Character.Effects: Specifies the effects for the character
Character.Alpha: Transparency of the character, ranging from 0 to 1
Character.RenderOrder: Render order of the character. Characters with larger values overlay those with smaller values
Character.StartPosition: Starting coordinate position for the character
Character.EndPosition: Ending coordinate position for the character
Character.MoveTime: Time for the character to move from the start position to the end position, in seconds
Character.MoveCurve: Curve type for the character movement

Choice.OutputVariable: Output variable name for the choice. The player's selection result will be stored in this variable

Delay.DelayTime: Duration of the delay, in seconds

VisualEffect.VisualEffectName: Specifies the effect name for the visual effect
VisualEffect.Steps: Specifies the duration of the visual effect in steps, i.e., how many controllers it lasts before terminating
VisualEffect.Position: Coordinate position of the visual effect
VisualEffect.RenderOrder: Render order of the visual effect. Effects with larger values overlay those with smaller values

Interlude.Character&Variant: Specifies the character name and variant for the interlude. If there is no variant, use only the character name
Interlude.SlotIndex: Specifies the slot index for the character in the interlude, default is 0. To display multiple characters simultaneously, use different slot indices to distinguish them.
Interlude.Type: Type of interlude. 0 for gray vertical type, 1 for avatar type
Interlude.IsBlocker: Whether to block. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line
Interlude.Effects: Specifies the effects for the interlude
Interlude.RenderOrder: Render order of the interlude. Interludes with larger values overlay those with smaller values

ColorMask.Color: Color of the color mask, using a 4-dimensional vector
ColorMask.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the color mask, in seconds
ColorMask.StayTime: Specifies the duration of the color mask, in seconds
ColorMask.FadeOutTime: Specifies the fade-out time for the color mask, in seconds
ColorMask.IsBlocker: Whether to block. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line

Shake.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the screen shake, in seconds
Shake.StayTime: Specifies the duration of the screen shake, in seconds
Shake.FadeOutTime: Specifies the fade-out time for the screen shake, in seconds
Shake.IsBlocker: Whether to block. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line

FreeText.IsAddition: Whether this is additional text. Additional text appends to existing text rather than replacing it
FreeText.Immediate: Whether to display text immediately instead of character by character
FreeText.IsBlocker: Whether to block. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line
FreeText.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the text, in seconds
FreeText.StayTime: Specifies the duration of the text, in seconds
FreeText.FadeOutTime: Specifies the fade-out time for the text, in seconds
FreeText.SlotIndex: Specifies the slot index for the text, default is 0. To display multiple texts simultaneously, use different slot indices to distinguish them.
FreeText.AsSeceneObject: Whether to treat the text as a scene object, making it affected by other scene controllers such as screen shake and color mask
FreeText.RenderOrder: Render order of the text. Texts with larger values overlay those with smaller values
FreeText.Interval: Time interval between the appearance of each character when displaying character by character, in seconds
FreeText.ExtraStayTime: Extra duration after the full text is displayed when displaying character by character, in seconds

Cut.Angle: Angle of the cut, in degrees
Cut.SlotIndex: Specifies the slot index for the cut, default is 0. To use multiple cuts simultaneously, use different slot indices to distinguish them.
Cut.IsBlocker: Whether to block. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line
Cut.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the cut, in seconds
Cut.StayTime: Specifies the duration of the cut, in seconds
Cut.FadeOutTime: Specifies the fade-out time for the cut, in seconds
Cut.RenderOrder: Render order of the cut. Cuts with larger values overlay those with smaller values

Filter.Type&Intensity: Specifies the filter type and intensity. Different filter types have different intensity ranges
Filter.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the filter, in seconds
Filter.IsBlocker: Whether to block. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line

AnimatedText.Type: Type of animated text. Different types have different animation effects
AnimatedText.IsBlocker: Whether to block. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line

Variable.Name: Variable name
Variable.Expression: Variable expression. The variable's value will evaluate to the result of this expression

Judge.Name: Variable name where the judgment result will be stored
Judge.Expression: Judgment expression. If the evaluation result is greater than 0, it will be stored in the variable

Branch.Name: Branch name
Branch.Branches: Names of the labels to jump to

Dialog.Speaker&Content: Speaker name and dialog text content
Dialog.IsAdditional: Whether this is additional dialog. Additional dialog appends to existing dialog rather than replacing it
Dialog.ModifyCharacters: Whether to synchronize existing character sprite displays to match speaker changes in the dialog, causing character sprites to automatically brighten/dim as the dialog progresses
Dialog.BasicStayTime: Base duration of the dialog, in seconds. The dialog will have additional time added based on text length on top of this base duration
Dialog.Interval: Time interval between the appearance of each character when displaying character by character, in seconds
Dialog.ExtraStayTime: Extra duration after the full text is displayed when displaying character by character, in seconds

Comment.__description: Comment line, not executed by the playback engine.

window.title: ASE-Remake Story Project Initialization Assistant
window.where: The new project will be created in "%1".
window.exists: "%1" already exists or is not empty! Cannot create a new project here.

failed.title: Failed to Create Project
failed.text: Unable to create project. Please check whether the target folder is empty or whether you have permission to create new files in this folder.

name.title: Project Name
name.text: The name of the story project. When using only path-legal characters, this will also be the name of the project folder.

path.title: Project Path
path.text: A new project folder will be created under this path

version.title: Target ASE Version
version.text: The latest ASE version is used by default. If you need to use a specific older version of ASE, please associate the relevant version in the plugin settings.

window.title: AStoryX File Initialization Assistant
window.where: The new file will be created as "%1".
window.exists: "%1" already exists! Cannot create a new file.

failed.title: Failed to Create File
failed.text: Unable to create file. Please check whether the target folder is empty or whether you have permission to create new files in this folder.

astory.name: ASE Story File
astory.description: ASE story written with AStoryX, supporting all ASE native features.
astory.title: AStoryX File Initialization

name.title: File Name
name.text: The name of the story file. When using only path-legal characters, this will also be the name of the file.

path.title: File Path
path.text: A new story file will be created under this path

version.title: Target ASE Version
version.text: The latest ASE version is used by default. If you need to use a specific older version of ASE, please associate the relevant version in the plugin settings.

exePath.title: Executable Path
exePath.text: The path to the ASE-Remake executable file.

version.title: ASE Version
version.text: The version of the ASE program you have selected. If you are unsure of your ASE program's version, or if your ASE program is the latest version, select "Latest Version". Correct version settings allow ASER Studio to better interface with your ASE program and avoid potential issues.

windowSize.title: Window Size
windowSize.text: ASER Studio can specify window size options when launching ASE-Remake. These options will be passed directly to the ASE-Remake program.

executableNotFound.title: Executable Not Found
executableNotFound.message: The configured path %1 is invalid and cannot launch the ASE-Remake application. Please check your configuration in File -> Plugins & Preferences -> ASER Studio Settings to ensure you have correctly set the path to the ASE-Remake executable.

starting.title: ASE-Remake Beta Defect Notice
starting.message: Due to a known defect in the ASE-Remake beta version, YSS can currently only launch ASE-Remake in a simple manner, unable to correctly set the project directory and playback file. You will need to manually start playback. Starting the debugger now can only be used as a quick way to open the ASE-Remake program. Please monitor the ASE-Remake beta changelog for updates on when this issue is fixed.
